assessment_items.question_content:
***The Gates* remains a complex testament to two controversial topics in contemporary art: where to create public art and how to decorate and beautify cities.**

![Christo and Jeanne-Claude, The Gates, 1979–2005 (view across the pond looking southeast) (photo: Wolfgang Volz) © 2005 Christo and Jeanne-Claude Foundation](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cb9f26d41bc82d3cd51a0d4124a82f99.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
*****The Gates* remains a complex testament to two controversial topics in contemporary art: how to create meaningful public art and how art responds to and impacts our relationship with the built environment..**

**[Read this essay to review](/test-prep/ap-art-history/global-contemporary/a/christo-and-jeanne-claude-the-gates)**

assessment_items.question_content:
***The Gates* was in line with other projects by Christo and Jeanne-Claude. Which of the following projects was NOT by these artists?**

![Christo and Jeanne-Claude, The Gates, 1979–2005 (view across the pond looking southeast) (photo: Wolfgang Volz) © 2005 Christo and Jeanne-Claude Foundation](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cb9f26d41bc82d3cd51a0d4124a82f99.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
***Levitated Mass* is by artist Michael Heizer and does not fit the kind of projects that Christo and Jeanne-Claude engaged in. 

**[Read this essay to review](/test-prep/ap-art-history/global-contemporary/a/christo-and-jeanne-claude-the-gates)**

choices.content: Surrounded Islands ![Surrounded Islands, Biscayne Bay, Greater Miami, Florida, 1980–83](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a9ca9436e9a3f70caa7d7a14ae3e11e3.jpg)
Levitated Mass ![Levitated Mass, 2012, diorite granite and concrete, 10.67 x 138.98 x 6.6 m (Los Angeles County Museum of Art)](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cec62aa884c1522dee4612b165000bec.jpg)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Christo and Jeanne-Claude only left *The Gates* up for two weeks because New York City planners would not allow them any more time.**

![Christo and Jeanne-Claude, The Gates, 1979–2005 (view across the pond looking southeast) (photo: Wolfgang Volz) © 2005 Christo and Jeanne-Claude Foundation](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cb9f26d41bc82d3cd51a0d4124a82f99.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**The artists said, “The temporary quality of the projects is an aesthetic decision. Our works are temporary in order to endow the works of art with a feeling of urgency to be seen, and the love and tenderness brought by the fact that they will not last." (Christo and Jeanne-Claude, NYC.gov, 2005)**

**[Read this essay to review](/test-prep/ap-art-history/global-contemporary/a/christo-and-jeanne-claude-the-gates)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is NOT true of *The Gates*?**


![Christo and Jeanne-Claude, The Gates, 1979–2005 (view across the pond looking southeast) (photo: Wolfgang Volz) © 2005 Christo and Jeanne-Claude Foundation](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cb9f26d41bc82d3cd51a0d4124a82f99.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Christo and Jeanne-Claude financed the project themselves and that the installation was free to the public.**

**[Read this essay to review](/test-prep/ap-art-history/global-contemporary/a/christo-and-jeanne-claude-the-gates)**

choices.content: It covered 23 miles of walkways in Central Park in New York City.
Christo and Jeanne-Claude were inspired by the way the city’s pedestrians navigate its paths.
There is no starting point and no end point and moreover, no favored point from which to view the work.
Christo and Jeanne-Claude financed the project with numerous grants, assistance from the City of New York, and ticket sales.
